texto: A jellyfish, if you watch it long enough, begins to look like a heart beating. It doesn't matter what kind: the blooded Atolla with its flashing siren lights, the frilly flower hat variety, or the near-transparent moon jelly, Aurelia aurita. It's their pulse, the way they contract swiftly, than release. Like a ghost heart-- a heart you can see right through, right into some other world where everything you ever lost as gone to hide. Jellyfish don't even have hearts, of course-- no heart, no brain, no bone, no blood. But watch them for a while. You will see them beating.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ali Benjamin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “A jellyfish, if you watch it long enough, begins to look like a heart beating. It doesn't matter …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, interesting, jellyfish pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e jellyfish.

frase_original: A jellyfish, if you watch it long enough, begins to look like a heart beating. It doesn't matter what kind: the blooded Atolla with its flashing siren lights, the frilly flower hat variety, or the near-transparent moon jelly, Aurelia aurita. It's their pulse, the way they contract swiftly, than release. Like a ghost heart-- a heart you can see right through, right into some other world where everything you ever lost as gone to hide. Jellyfish don't even have hearts, of course-- no heart, no brain, no bone, no blood. But watch them for a while. You will see them beating.

texto: If people were silent, they could hear the noise of their own lives better. If people were silent, it would make what they did say, whenever they chose to say it, more important. If people were silent, they could read one another's signals, the way underwater creatures flash lights at one another, or turn their skin different colors.
explicacao: Citação de Ali Benjamin sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “If people were silent, they could hear the noise of their own lives better. If people were silent…” articula reflexao, silence, people em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e reflexao.
